meta.name: EirEval v0.1
meta.description: An evaluation of LLM knowledge of Irish and EU financial regulation. Multiple choice with abstention. Every item cites its source instrument. Items are marked verified:false until checked against the source text by a human.
meta.author: Navina Ganapathy Amuthan

questions.question: Under EU distance marketing rules for financial services, what is the standard cooling-off period during which a consumer can withdraw from a distance contract for most financial products (excluding life assurance and pensions)?

source.instrument: Directive 2002/65/EC (Distance Marketing of Consumer Financial Services)

questions.question: The Central Bank of Ireland's Code of Conduct on Mortgage Arrears (CCMA) and its Mortgage Arrears Resolution Process (MARP) apply to arrears on which type of property?

options.A: Any mortgaged property in the State
options.D: Commercial property with a personal guarantee

source.instrument: Code of Conduct on Mortgage Arrears (CBI, 2013)

questions.question: The Central Bank's Consumer Protection Code requires that all advertising by regulated firms be:

options.A: Approved in advance by the Central Bank
options.B: Clear, fair, accurate and not misleading
options.C: Accompanied by a risk warning in all cases
options.D: Published only through licensed media channels

questions.question: Under the Consumer Protection Code 2012, within how many business days must a regulated firm acknowledge a consumer complaint in writing?
An Irish consumer whose complaint against a bank is not resolved through the firm's internal process may escalate it to which statutory body?

options.A: The Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (CCPC)
options.B: The Financial Services and Pensions Ombudsman (FSPO)

source.instrument: Financial Services and Pensions Ombudsman Act 2017

questions.question: Under the Central Bank's Code of Conduct on the Switching of Payment Accounts, once a switch is initiated, the new provider must complete the switching process within:

source.instrument: Code of Conduct on the Switching of Payment Accounts with Payment Service Providers (CBI)

questions.question: MiFID II requires investment firms to categorise clients into which three categories?
Under MiFID II, a suitability assessment (rather than a mere appropriateness assessment) is required when a firm provides:
MiFID II changed the best-execution standard from MiFID I's wording. Firms must now take:

options.A: All reasonable steps to obtain the best possible result
options.B: All sufficient steps to obtain the best possible result
options.C: All practicable steps to obtain the best available price
options.D: Commercially reasonable efforts to execute promptly

questions.question: As introduced by MiFID II, investment research provided by third parties to portfolio managers must generally be:

options.A: Free of charge to all clients
options.B: Paid for separately from execution commissions (unbundled)
options.D: Limited to firms with over €1bn AUM

questions.question: Under MiFID II's execution-only regime, which of the following is generally treated as a NON-complex instrument that can be sold without an appropriateness test?

options.B: Shares admitted to trading on a regulated market

questions.question: Which MiFID II client category receives the LOWEST level of investor protection?
Ireland's primary anti-money-laundering legislation is:

options.A: The Criminal Justice (Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing) Act 2010, as amended

source.instrument: Criminal Justice (Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing) Act 2010

questions.question: Under Irish AML law, customer due diligence is required for an occasional transaction (outside a business relationship) at or above what threshold?
What level of due diligence must a designated person apply to a politically exposed person (PEP)?

options.D: No additional measures if the PEP is domestic

source.instrument: CJA (MLTF) Act 2010 (as amended by 2018 Act, implementing 4AMLD/5AMLD)

questions.question: A distinctive feature of Ireland's suspicious transaction reporting regime is that STRs must be filed with:

options.A: The Financial Intelligence Unit (FIU Ireland) only
options.B: The Central Bank of Ireland only
options.C: Both FIU Ireland (An Garda Síochána) and the Revenue Commissioners

questions.question: For Ireland's Register of Beneficial Ownership (RBO), a beneficial owner is generally a natural person holding more than what percentage of shares or voting rights?

source.instrument: EU (Anti-Money Laundering: Beneficial Ownership of Corporate Entities) Regulations 2019 (SI 110/2019)

questions.question: Informing a customer that a suspicious transaction report has been or may be made about them is:

options.A: Permitted if done in good faith

questions.question: The Irish Deposit Guarantee Scheme protects eligible deposits up to what amount per depositor, per institution?
Strong Customer Authentication under PSD2 requires at least two independent elements from which categories?
Under PSD2, a payer's maximum liability for losses from an unauthorised payment made with a lost or stolen instrument (absent fraud or gross negligence) is capped at:
For SEPA direct debits, a consumer has an unconditional 'no questions asked' refund right for an authorised debit within:

source.instrument: SEPA Core Direct Debit Scheme Rulebook; PSD2
source.provision: Refund right for authorised transactions (PSD2 Art 76-77)

questions.question: Under Ireland's Central Credit Register, lenders are obliged to check a borrower's CCR report for loan applications at or above:
The standard contactless payment limit per transaction in Ireland (without SCA step-up) is:

source.instrument: Industry limit under PSD2 SCA exemptions (RTS (EU) 2018/389 Art 11)

questions.question: Under the Central Bank's Fitness & Probity regime, appointments to which category of role require pre-approval by the Central Bank before appointment?
SEAR, introduced by Ireland's Individual Accountability Framework, stands for:
The Minimum Competency Code applies to persons who:

options.A: Hold any role in a regulated firm
options.B: Provide advice or information on, or arrange, retail financial products for consumers

questions.question: The Central Bank's Fitness & Probity Standards require that persons in controlled functions be:

options.B: Competent and capable; honest, ethical and act with integrity; financially sound
options.D: Approved by the firm's external auditor

questions.question: Under the Single Supervisory Mechanism, 'significant' Irish credit institutions are directly supervised by:

options.A: The Central Bank of Ireland alone

questions.question: Firms that service loan books sold by banks to non-bank purchasers ('credit servicing firms') in Ireland must be:

options.B: Authorised by the Central Bank of Ireland
options.C: Licensed by the Department of Housing
options.D: Members of the Banking & Payments Federation

source.instrument: Consumer Protection (Regulation of Credit Servicing Firms) Acts 2015 and 2018

questions.question: Under the EU AI Act, AI systems used to evaluate the creditworthiness of natural persons or establish their credit score are classified as:

options.D: Out of scope for financial services

questions.question: GDPR Article 22 gives individuals the right not to be subject to:

options.A: Any processing of financial data without consent
options.B: Decisions based solely on automated processing that produce legal or similarly significant effects

questions.question: Most obligations for high-risk AI systems under the EU AI Act (Annex III) become applicable in:
The maximum administrative fine under the EU AI Act for engaging in prohibited AI practices is:
DORA, applicable to EU financial entities from January 2025, primarily governs:

options.B: ICT risk management and digital operational resilience

questions.question: Under GDPR, a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) is mandatory when processing is:

options.A: Carried out by any financial institution
options.B: Likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons
options.C: Performed on more than 1,000 data subjects
